CVX.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 2026 earnings are imminent (April 24-May 1 window; today is April 30, so earnings are either today/tomorrow or just reported). The setup is a classic pre-catalyst exhaustion/sell-the-news risk. Net news sentiment: mixed-negative. Wells Fargo upgraded to $222 post-April 8 selloff (positive), but the Q1 print is expected to reflect $2.7-3.7B in Middle East commodity damage with consensus EPS at $1.50 — a low bar that may still disappoint on revenue/production. Oil ceasefire has already reversed the energy risk premium. Insider activity: net selling — insider Pate sold $8.6M; no offsetting buys. Technical setup going in: extended and deteriorating. Hourly RSI at 76.18 (overbought), hourly RSI bearish divergence confirmed, price at 92% of hourly Bollinger band, daily OBV trend is DOWN and diverging from price (bearish accumulation/distribution). Daily ADX 32.24 but minus_di (29.97) exceeds plus_di (21.31), indicating bears still control the daily directional trend despite the recent 3-day bounce. The bounce from $183.67 to $193 has occurred without volume confirmation (relative volume 0.77, below average). Z-score 120 at 1.63 on the hourly signals short-term overextension. Wiki confirms the structural thesis: the ceasefire ended the Hormuz disruption premium; the upcoming earnings will crystallize the $2.7-3.7B hit. Analyst consensus at $206 provides a floor, but the near-term catalyst is bearish. Selling into the earnings event with the extended technical setup is the higher-probability trade.

WMT.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 FY2027 earnings on May 21, 2026 — approximately 15 trading days out, just beyond the ideal 10-day window but significant as a pre-earnings positioning catalyst. Net news sentiment: mixed-positive. Guggenheim raised to $137, analyst consensus $136 (Strong Buy, 32/39 recs). First-ever profitable e-commerce quarter globally, US comps +4.5%. Tariff uncertainty (US-China 145%) is a structural headwind but already known. Negative: Walton family sold $4.6B (significant insider distribution signal — the founding family treating current price as a distribution opportunity). HSBC downgraded Feb 2026. Technical setup going in: constructive. Daily EMA stack fully aligned bullish (ema_aligned_uptrend: true). Price pulled back from $132.46 recent high to $128, testing the 20-EMA ($127.22) and has bounced. Daily RSI 14 at 46.79 (room to run, not overbought). MACD histogram positive. Hourly ADX 30.25 with plus_di (36.33) far ahead of minus_di (14.64) — strong bullish trend on the hourly. Hourly OBV trending up, no divergence. Relative volume 1.4x on hourly — elevated, suggesting informed positioning into the earnings window. Hourly RSI 70.29 is elevated but no bearish divergence. The 20-day SMA/EMA cluster ($127-128) is acting as support. Confidence is limited to 52 due to: (1) earnings are 15 trading days away (outside ideal window), (2) Walton insider selling at scale is a meaningful distribution signal, and (3) the stock is already up 12-14% YTD with the defensive trade-down thesis largely priced in. Entry on the current pullback, targeting analyst consensus $135-136 pre-earnings.
